claims.text: Pacific Fusion is a rival of Helion.
Pacific Fusion's prototype reactor sustained a net-energy-producing plasma for 41 seconds.
If confirmed, Pacific Fusion's 41-second net-energy-producing plasma would be the longest confinement of its kind.
The plasma shot from Pacific Fusion's prototype reactor produced 1.9 times the energy injected into the fuel.
Pacific Fusion's reported figure of 1.9 times energy gain excludes the power consumed by the reactor's magnets and cooling systems.
Independent researchers urged caution about Pacific Fusion's net-energy claim.
Pacific Fusion's claimed energy gain would be a genuine milestone if the diagnostics hold up.
claims.stance_source: Dr. Ingrid Halvorsen of the Max Planck Institute

claims.text: Pacific Fusion has not yet released calorimetry data for its net-energy shot.
claims.stance_source: Dr. Ingrid Halvorsen of the Max Planck Institute

claims.text: Pacific Fusion has not yet submitted its net-energy-shot results for peer review.
claims.stance_source: Dr. Ingrid Halvorsen of the Max Planck Institute

claims.text: Previous private-sector fusion claims have shrunk substantially under scrutiny.
A 2024 fusion announcement by another startup was later revised downward by a factor of three.
Pacific Fusion plans to begin construction of a pilot power plant in 2027.
Pacific Fusion plans to deliver electricity to the grid by 2032.
Most academic roadmaps place grid-connected fusion no earlier than the 2040s.
If Pacific Fusion's timeline holds, it would compress the standard academic projection for grid-connected fusion by more than a decade.
The gap between Pacific Fusion's timeline and standard academic projections reflects marketing pressure from an oversubscribed funding round rather than any physics breakthrough.

theses.text: Pacific Fusion has announced a record-setting net-energy fusion result — a 41-second plasma producing 1.9 times the injected energy — that would be a milestone if confirmed but whose reported gain excludes the reactor's magnet and cooling power.

Independent experts caution that Pacific Fusion's result is unverified, since the company has released no calorimetry data and sought no peer review, and previous private-sector fusion claims have shrunk substantially under scrutiny.

Pacific Fusion's plan to deliver grid electricity by 2032 runs more than a decade ahead of mainstream academic fusion roadmaps, a gap skeptics attribute to funding-round marketing pressure rather than a physics breakthrough.
